accurate.why: All claims match the bill text and the contextual references (Section 1013, War Powers Resolution, Section 601(b)) are factually correct and verified.

no_hallucinations.why: No fabricated claims; the sponsor, statutes, dates, and casualty figures all match the bill.

states_purpose.why: Clearly identifies the bill's purpose of directing removal of U.S. Armed Forces from unauthorized hostilities against Iran.

changes_to_existing_law.why: Correctly notes the invocation of Section 1013 and Section 601(b) expedited procedures, though this resolution directs action rather than amending statutes.

exceptions_conditions.why: Accurately captures the rule-of-construction carve-outs (self-defense, intelligence sharing, partner assistance, evacuation) and the declaration-of-war/AUMF exception.

effective_dates.why: The bill specifies no separate effective date and the summary correctly notes this.

major_provisions_covered.why: Captures the operative removal directive and the rule-of-construction exceptions faithfully.

objective_tone.why: Neutral, non-partisan reporting of findings and provisions with quoted findings attributed as findings.

coherent.why: Well-organized and internally consistent, moving logically from purpose to findings to operative provisions to exceptions.

concise.why: Free of padding or repetition while covering the essential content.

correct_entities.why: Correctly names Mr. Van Hollen, the War Powers Resolution, Section 1013, and Section 601(b) of the International Security Assistance and Arms Export Control Act of 1976.

correct_figures.why: Dates (Feb 28, March 2, April 8, 2026) and casualty figures (13 killed, 380 wounded) all match the bill text.
